dialogos.texto_en: Mmm, this is delicious!\nChoosing this was the right answer.
Yeah, I can just keep eating this flavor forever.
What are you eating?
Oh, it's Uesugi-kun.
Taco rice.\nThey were selling it at the food truck over there.
...Looks delicious.
Uesugi-kun, do you want to eat it too?\nI won't give you mine, though.
No need to be so threatening.\nI won't take yours, so relax.
I won't give you mine either.
What do you guys think of me...?
Since you're here, why don't you get some too,\nFutaro?\nOkinawa has the real stuff.
Yes, along with soki soba noodles and goya champuru stir-fry,\nit's one of the representative dishes of Okinawa.
Even on Kokusai dori,\nI saw some stores selling taco rice.
Actually, I didn't know about that\nuntil I came to Okinawa.
Really? That's surprising.\nI thought it was well known.
Well, tacos are Mexican food,\nso I was wondering if taco rice was also.
Heheh, I know the answer here.
Tacos are when the ingredients are wrapped in a tortilla.\nBut%p-2;――
The idea of putting those ingredients on rice\nwas born in Okinawa!
Taco rice is a completely new dish\nthat was created based on Mexican food,\ntacos, in Okinawa.
I see, that's what it is.
You know a lot, Itsuki.
I studied in advance.\nIf you want, I can tell you more.
Like where it was born in Okinawa,\nand how it spread!
That's okay for now...
*sigh*\nTalking about them like this,\nI'm starting to want tacos too.
Aren't you already eating them?
Uesugi-kun? Were you listening to what I said?\nThis is taco rice, not tacos.
Well, that's true, but\nthey're pretty similar, aren't they?
Although the flavor profile is similar,\nthey're completely different dishes!
O-okay, my bad.
You don't think that's the point, do you, Uesugi-kun?\nPlease pay more attention to food.
It's hard to take you seriously when you say it like that.
What's that supposed to mean?!
...Heheh!
Ah...\nExcuse me, Miku.
No need to apologize.
Itsuki, things are back to normal.\nI feel relieved.
Huh?
Futaro, you feel the same way, right?
No comment.\nIf I were to say anything, Itsuki might get angry.
Does that mean you're thinking of something\nthat might make her angry?
...No comment.
I feel relieved when I see Itsuki enjoying her meals,\njust like always.
Her smile at those times is a symbol of our peace.
I-isn't that a bit too exaggerated...
Yet, it's true. You should also try\npracticing cooking, instead of just eating.
Uungh...\nI guess I leave the cooking to you and Nino a little too much.
Yeah, with how prepared she came.\nItsuki is pretty serious.
That's true, but I think there's one more thing.
Well, yeah, I'd say it's being able\nto enjoy food without any worries.
Yeah, that's it. I agree.
...I'll take it as a compliment.
I feel relieved when I see Itsuki enjoying her meals,\njust like always.
Her smile at those times is a symbol of our peace.
I-isn't that a bit too exaggerated...
Yet, it's true. You should also try\npracticing cooking, instead of just eating.
Uungh...\nI guess I leave the cooking to you and Nino a little too much.
Oh yeah, very relieved.\nWhen I see Itsuki's eyes shine at the sight of food.
Hmm...
Ah, I see.\nThen I feel relieved\nwhen you start spouting trivia, you know?
Really now? How about I tell you some Mexican history\nto go with your taco trivia?
I'll pass. I'll feel like I'm in class.
But it's impressive that you could\ncounter Futaro like that.
Teehee!\nI haven't just been sitting here\ngetting talked at, you know.
I feel relieved when I see you enjoying her meals,\njust like always.
Her smile at those times is a symbol of our peace.
I-isn't that a bit too exaggerated...
Yet, it's true. You should also try\npracticing cooking, instead of just eating.
Uungh...\nI guess I leave the cooking to you and Nino a little too much.
I think there will come a day\nwhen you want to treat someone to a home-cooked meal.
Wait, what are you talking about?
...Yes, you're right. I'll keep that in mind.
Huh?
Don't worry about it.\nIt's not about you.
Nah, that does make me wonder...
